evidence: The docstring asks whether the sumset A + B of two dense subsets of {1,...,N} must contain a composite number.
verify_note: decl green_58 exists (research open); sumset A+B is the object of the question

evidence: The statement asks 'Must the sumset A + B contain a composite number?' (m.Composite in the Lean statement).
verify_note: decl exists; m.Composite is the literal question asked

evidence: The problem asks whether the sumset A + A of a set of squares satisfies |A + A| >= |A|^{1+c}.
verify_note: decl green_60 exists (research open); |A+A| growth is the statement

evidence: The hypothesis is that A is a set of squares ('whenever A is a set of squares with |A| >= 2', IsSquare a in Lean).
verify_note: decl exists; hypothesis IsSquare a on all elements of A

evidence: The docstring supposes the sumset A + A contains the first n squares and asks whether |A| >= n^{1-o(1)}.
verify_note: decl green_61 exists (research open); A+A containing squares is the hypothesis

evidence: The hypothesis is that A + A contains the first n squares ((Finset.Icc 1 n).image (· ^ 2) in the Lean statement).
verify_note: decl exists; (Icc 1 n).image (.^2) = first n squares, load-bearing

evidence: The docstring lets p be a large prime and A be the set of all primes less than p (Erdos-Odlyzko-Sarkozy 1987).
verify_note: decl green_62 exists (research open); p prime and A = primes < p, both central

evidence: The question is whether every x in {1,...,p-1} is congruent to some product a1*a2 modulo p (stated via ZMod p in Lean).
verify_note: decl exists; congruence mod p via ZMod p is the statement's relation

evidence: The problem asks whether there is always a sum of two squares between X - X^{1/4}/10 and X.
verify_note: decl green_66 exists (research open); IsSumOfTwoSquares n = a^2+b^2, squares load-bearing

evidence: The solved variant records the well-known O(X^{1/4}) bound for gaps between sums of two squares.
verify_note: decl green_66.variants.trivial_bound exists (research solved); same sum-of-two-squares content

evidence: The docstring asks 'Does Ulam's sequence have positive density?', stated via Erdos342.IsUlamSequence.
verify_note: decl green_7.variants.positive_density exists (research open); Ulam sequence via Erdos342.IsUlamSequence is the subject

evidence: The question is whether the Ulam sequence has positive (upper) density, via Set.upperDensity in the Lean statement.
verify_note: decl exists; positive (upper) density is the literal question, Set.upperDensity

evidence: The file header says this problem is 'more commonly known as the no-three-in-line problem', the docstring labels green_72 'Green's Open Problem 72 / No-three-in-line problem', and the file declares alias no_three_in_line := green_72.
verify_note: decl green_72 exists (research open) with alias no_three_in_line; states NoKInLineFor 3 N for all N>=3 = exactly the no-three-in-line conjecture; QID label matches

evidence: NoKInLine states the no-k-in-line generalization of the no-three-in-line problem for all k > 2.
verify_note: decl NoKInLine exists (research open); no-k-in-line generalization, covered by the no-three-in-line article; mentions ok

evidence: This variant asks whether the no-three-in-line problem holds for all sufficiently large N.
verify_note: decl green_72.variants.eventually exists (research open); asymptotic form of the same problem

evidence: Records the computer verification of the no-three-in-line problem for N <= 60.
verify_note: decl no_three_in_line_le exists (research solved); computer verification N<=60 of the same problem

evidence: Records the Grebennikov-Kwan (arXiv:2510.17743) proof of the no-k-in-line conjecture for k > 10^37, a generalization of the no-three-in-line problem.
verify_note: decl no_k_in_line_big exists (research solved); Grebennikov-Kwan no-k-in-line for k>10^37, generalization discussed on the page; mentions ok

evidence: The docstring asks whether n points in the unit disc must determine a triangle of area at most n^{-2+o(1)}, the central open question of the Heilbronn triangle problem, stated via the Erdos507 minimal-triangle-area function alpha.
verify_note: decl green_77 exists (research open); states Delta(n)=n^{-2+o(1)} via Erdos507 alpha, the central open question the Heilbronn triangle problem item denotes; formalizes ok
